target.name: XMOS
target.founder: Ali Dixon, James Foster, Noel Hurley, David May, Hitesh Mehta

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
XMOS is a growth-stage semiconductor company that has raised £200M+ to date. The peer universe consists of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor—a total of 11 companies across Chips & Semiconductors.

Two standard valuation approaches were assessed for applicability. Peer-Median analysis proved inapplicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, the filtered set (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making a median calculation unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring using the 3 public semiconductor tickers could not be executed because XMOS's revenue is not on file—a common constraint for private companies. The public Semiconductors sector P/S median stands at 24.2x across the 3 public comps, which would have served as a useful anchor were revenue disclosed.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported from the available data. A credible analysis would require either: (i) disclosure of XMOS's revenue to enable public-comp anchoring, or (ii) access to transaction multiples or recent fundraising rounds among the identified private peer set to construct a narrow, stage-matched comparable. The lack of both inputs represents the binding constraint on this valuation exercise.
